title: Oscillators from non-semisimple walled Brauer algebras
abstract: The walled Brauer algebras $B_N(m,n)$ govern Schur--Weyl duality for unitary groups $U(N)$ acting on mixed tensor spaces $V_N^{\otimes m}\otimes \overline{V}_N^{\otimes n}$ and play an important role in applications ranging from AdS/CFT to quantum information theory. In the stable regime $N\ge m+n$ the algebra is semisimple and its representation theory is well understood. For $N<m+n$, however, $B_N(m,n)$ becomes non-semisimple. The representation of the algebra on tensor space has a non-trivial kernel and the corresponding quotient algebra is semisimple, with representation dimensions differing from those in the stable regime.   We introduce \emph{restricted Bratteli diagrams}, obtained by modifying the standard Bratteli diagrams for $B_N(m,n)$. This construction provides a systematic way to use representation-theoretic data from the stable regime to compute the dimension modifications arising in the non-semisimple regime. In the regime $N=m+n-l$, with $l$ small compared to $m,n$, we show that the restricted diagrams exhibit a stability property and enable an efficient counting of the paths responsible for these dimension corrections.   Remarkably, the resulting generating functions are governed by the partition function of an infinite tower of simple harmonic oscillators. We briefly discuss implications for the construction of orthogonal bases of matrix invariants in gauge theory and related applications in quantum information theory.
summary: The paper provides a new method to calculate representation dimensions for walled Brauer algebras in non-semisimple regimes using modified Bratteli diagrams. It reveals a surprising connection between these algebraic dimension corrections and the partition functions of simple harmonic oscillators. This has implications for constructing matrix invariants in gauge theory and quantum information theory.
main_problem: The paper addresses the representation theory of non-semisimple walled Brauer algebras B_N(m,n) in the regime where N < m + n, specifically how to compute dimension modifications in this regime.
main_result: The authors introduce restricted Bratteli diagrams to systematically compute dimension corrections and show that the resulting generating functions are governed by the partition function of an infinite tower of simple harmonic oscillators.
method: The authors utilize a modification of standard Bratteli diagrams (restricted Bratteli diagrams) to track representation-theoretic data from the stable regime to the non-semisimple regime.
model_or_system: The study focuses on walled Brauer algebras B_N(m,n) acting on mixed tensor spaces V_N^{tensor m} \otimes \overline{V}_N^{tensor n}, which are relevant to Schur-Weyl duality for unitary groups U(N).
key_observables: Representation dimensions and the partition function of the resulting oscillator system.
important_parameters: The regime N < m + n, specifically the case N = m + n - l where l is small compared to m and n.
main_assumptions: The analysis focuses on the stability property of restricted diagrams in the regime where the deviation from the stable regime (l) is small.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of non-semisimple walled Brauer algebras, presents the construction of restricted Bratteli diagrams, demonstrates a stability property for small l, identifies the connection to harmonic oscillator partition functions, and discusses applications to gauge theory and quantum information.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
